sanskrit:
सर्वकर्माण्यपि सदा कुर्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः।
मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम्॥
transliteration:
sarva-karmāṇy api sadā kurvāṇo mad-vyapāśrayaḥ |
mat-prasādād avāpnoti śāśvataṁ padam avyayam ||
literal_meaning: All actions—even always performing—taking shelter in Me; by My grace attains the eternal, imperishable state.
contextual_meaning: A person who feels protected by an inner moral compass can remain active in the world without being defined by it, eventually arriving at a lasting sense of self-coherence.
psychological_state: Secure functioning: the nervous system stays regulated because actions are anchored in an internalized safe other, allowing sustained engagement without burnout.
trigger_scenario: A caregiver juggling work, parenting, and aging parents who suddenly realizes, 'I don’t have to fix everything; I just have to stay connected to what matters,' and feels a wave of calm.
anchor_text: Act always, trusting the quiet compass within; its steady guide leads you home to your deathless self.
